title: pteridaceae
intro: Pteridaceae is a family of plants commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. It is a taxonomic rank that groups plants with similar characteristics, such as the presence of fleshy or winged rhizomes, and the arrangement of fronds in a specific pattern. This family includes over 1,200 species of ferns, many of which are popular ornamental plants. The name Pteridaceae comes from the Greek word "pteris," meaning fern, highlighting the family's primary characteristics and composition.
